question: How many distinct triangles can be formed by selecting any three vertices of a hexagon?
Consider the set of digits {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}. If repetition is allowed digits, how many 4-digit numbers formed from this set will be even?
Consider a group of 5 boys and 3 girls. If they are to be seated in a single row, how many seating arrangements are possible if the girls must be separated?
A code consists of  letters using the word "MAGIC", with no repetition. How many such codes can be made such that 'A' is never at the beginning or end?
From the letters of the word ALGORITHM, how many -letter words (not necessarily meaningful) can be formed if the word must begin with a vowel and end with a consonant?
In how many ways can the letters of the word SCHOOL be arranged such that the vowels always come together?
In how many ways can the letters of the word "BANANA" be arranged?
A chef has 4 identical red plates, 3 identical blue plates, and 2 identical green plates. In how many distinct ways can he arrange all the 9 plates in a single row?

A club has 5 painters, 4 dancers, and 3 singers. A committee of 5 people is to be formed such that it includes at least 2 painters and at least 1 dancer, but not more than 1 singer. How many such committees are possible?
From a group of 8 males and 6 females, how many different 5-person committees can be assembled if there must be a minimum of 3 females on each committee?
With 10 points in a plane, and knowing that no three are in a straight line, what is the total count of triangles that can be constructed by connecting these points?
In a class of 10 students, how many ways can a president, vice-president, and secretary be selected (all different)?
A magician has 6 different tricks. He wants to perform 4 of them in a show, but two specific tricks should never be together. How many ways can he do this?
How many 3-digit numbers can be formed using digits 1, 2, 3, 4 without repeating any digit?
Consider a bag with 7 red and 5 blue balls. What is the number of ways to pick 4 balls such that a minimum of 3 of them are red?
